faq_page.meta_title: Frequently Asked Questions - Car Injury Clinic

hero.title: Frequently Asked Questions
hero.subtitle: Find answers to the most common questions about car accidents and personal injuries

intro.title: Do you have questions about your case?
intro.description: Here you'll find answers to the most frequently asked questions about car accidents, personal injuries, and the legal process. If you can't find the answer you're looking for, don't hesitate to contact us directly.

q1.question: How long do I have to file a car accident lawsuit?
q1.answer: In most states, you have between 2 and 3 years from the date of the accident to file a personal injury lawsuit. However, this timeframe can vary by state and the specific circumstances of your case. It's important to consult with an attorney as soon as possible to protect your rights.

q2.question: What should I do immediately after a car accident?
q2.answer: After an accident, it's crucial to: 1) Check for injuries and call 911 if necessary, 2) Document the scene with photos, 3) Exchange information with the other driver, 4) Get witness information, 5) Report the accident to your insurance company, and 6) Seek medical attention even if you don't feel pain immediately.

q3.question: How much could my car accident case be worth?
q3.answer: The value of your case depends on several factors: the severity of your injuries, medical expenses, lost income, pain and suffering, and the degree of fault of the other driver. Each case is unique, and an experienced attorney can evaluate your specific situation to give you a more accurate estimate.

q4.question: Do I need a lawyer for my car accident case?
q4.answer: While not mandatory, it's highly recommended to hire an attorney, especially if there are significant injuries, disputes about fault, or if the insurance company offers a low settlement. An attorney can maximize your compensation and handle the legal complexities while you recover.

q5.question: What happens if the other driver doesn't have insurance?
q5.answer: If the other driver doesn't have insurance, you still have options: you can use your uninsured motorist coverage (if you have it), file a lawsuit directly against the driver, or explore other sources of compensation. An attorney can help you identify all available options in your situation.

q6.question: How much does it cost to hire a car accident attorney?
q6.answer: Most car accident attorneys work on a contingency fee basis, which means they only get paid if they win your case. Typically, this represents between 33% and 40% of the final settlement. You pay nothing upfront, and if there's no recovery, there are no fees.

q7.question: What types of damages can I claim after a car accident?
q7.answer: You can claim economic damages (medical expenses, lost income, property damage) and non-economic damages (pain and suffering, emotional distress, loss of enjoyment of life). In cases of gross negligence, punitive damages may also apply.

q8.question: What if I was partially at fault for the accident?
q8.answer: Depending on your state's laws, you may still be entitled to compensation. Some states follow comparative negligence rules, where your compensation is reduced by your percentage of fault. Other states have stricter contributory negligence rules.

q9.question: How long does it take to resolve a car accident case?
q9.answer: The time varies significantly depending on the complexity of the case. Simple cases can be resolved in a few months, while complex cases with serious injuries can take 1-3 years or more. Your attorney can give you a more specific estimate based on your particular situation.

chiropractic.title: Chiropractic Therapy
chiropractic.description: Specific adjustments and gentle techniques to align the spine, reduce inflammation and improve mobility after an accident.

rehabilitation.title: Injury Rehabilitation
rehabilitation.description: Personalized rehabilitation plans that address the cause of your symptoms and accelerate functional recovery.

physical_therapy.title: Physical Therapy
physical_therapy.description: Guided exercises and manual techniques to relieve pain, strengthen muscles and prevent long-term complications.

hot_cold_therapy.title: Hot-Cold Therapy
hot_cold_therapy.description: Quick relief combining cold to reduce inflammation and heat to relax muscles and improve mobility. Ideal for sprains, bruises and stiffness caused by an accident.

massage.title: Massage Therapy
massage.description: Therapeutic massage to reduce muscle tension, improve circulation and complement your treatment.

ultrasound.title: Ultrasound Therapy
ultrasound.description: Therapeutic ultrasound that generates deep micro-massage and controlled heat to reduce pain, relax soft tissue and improve mobility.

tens.title: TENS Therapy (Electrostimulation)
tens.description: TENS is a non-invasive therapy that uses low-intensity electrical impulses on the skin to help modulate pain and relax muscles. It is effective support for post-accident discomfort such as neck pain, back pain and sprains, and integrates into your rehabilitation plan.

second_opinions.title: Second Opinions
second_opinions.description: Independent medical evaluation to confirm diagnoses, explore treatment alternatives and provide greater peace of mind to the patient and their attorney.

xray.title: Digital X-Rays
xray.description: High-quality digital images that allow rapid and accurate diagnosis of bone injuries after an accident.

mri.title: MRI Request and Results Reading
mri.description: Coordination of magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) and analysis of results by specialists for a complete view of internal damage.

pain_management.title: Pain Management Specialist
pain_management.description: Advanced medical treatments to control chronic pain, restore mobility and improve quality of life.

surgical_consultations.title: Surgical Consultations with Specialist
surgical_consultations.description: Evaluations with specialized surgeons to determine if surgery is necessary and coordinate the best care plan.

concussion.title: Concussion Diagnosis
concussion.description: Clinical evaluation to detect and treat concussions, ensuring proper and safe management after an accident.

free_transport.title: Free Transportation
free_transport.description: At Car Injury Clinic we understand that transportation can be an obstacle to caring for your health. That's why we offer free transportation for all our patients who don't have a way to get here. This way we guarantee that you attend your appointment comfortably, safely and without worries.

trilingual.title: Trilingual Care
trilingual.description: Our team serves you in Spanish, English and Portuguese, so you receive the best care without communication barriers.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.subtitle: At Car Injury Clinic, we protect your privacy and handle your personal information with the utmost care and transparency.
privacy.section1_content: At Car Injury Clinic, we are committed to protecting your privacy and handling your personal information responsibly. This privacy policy explains how we collect, use, store, and protect your information when you use our medical and legal services.
privacy.section2_item1_content: We collect information such as your name, address, phone number, email address, date of birth, medical insurance information, and details about your accident or injury.
privacy.section2_item2_content: We collect information about your medical history, symptoms, treatments, diagnoses, and any information relevant to your medical care.
privacy.section2_item3_content: We collect information about how you prefer to be contacted and when is the best time to reach you.
privacy.section3_item1_content: We use your information to provide you with quality medical care, coordinate treatments, and communicate with you about your care.
privacy.section3_item2_content: We use your information to provide legal assistance related to your accident or injury, including communication with insurance companies.
privacy.section3_item3_content: We use your contact information to schedule appointments, send reminders, and communicate with you about your treatment.
privacy.section4_content: We implement physical, technical, and administrative security measures to protect your personal information against unauthorized access, misuse, or disclosure. We comply with all HIPAA regulations and other applicable privacy laws.
privacy.section5_item1_content: You have the right to request access to the personal information we have about you and to receive a copy of it.
privacy.section5_item2_content: You may request that we correct any inaccurate or incomplete personal information we have about you.
privacy.section5_item3_content: Under certain circumstances, you may request that we delete your personal information, subject to our legal and medical obligations.
privacy.section6_item1_content: We may share your information with other healthcare providers involved in your care, such as specialists or laboratories.
privacy.section6_item2_content: We share necessary information with insurance companies for claims processing and treatment authorization.
privacy.section6_item3_content: We may disclose information when required by law, court order, or to protect public health and safety.
privacy.section7_content: We may update this privacy policy occasionally. We will notify you of significant changes and the updated version will be available on our website.
privacy.contact_content: If you have questions about this privacy policy or how we handle your personal information, please don't hesitate to contact us:
privacy.contact_address:
Car Injury Clinic
123 Main Street
Salt Lake City, UT 84101
United States
